headline: West Red Lake DRILLS 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen — Austin 904 Complex Rivals Historic Red Lake Highs
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSXV: WRLG) returned 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m — including 388.90 g/t over 2.95m — at the Austin 904 Complex in Ontario's Red Lake district. Results exceed the historic Goldcorp High-Grade Zone benchmark; production from this complex is targeted for H1 2027.

headline: Amex Exploration Posts C$1.13B NPV for Perron Gold Mine — 147,000 Oz/Yr at $910 AISC in Quebec Abitibi
snippet: Amex Exploration (TSXV: AMX) delivered a positive feasibility study for Phase 1 of the Perron Gold Mine in Abitibi, Quebec: 147,000 oz/yr over 5 years, C$1.13B post-tax NPV5 at 114.6% IRR, $910/oz AISC, and a low C$193.9M capex via toll milling. Revenue targeted by 2028.

headline: NexGen STARTS BUILDING Rook I This Summer — 30M Lb/Yr Uranium Output Would Top All of North America
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE) secured final CNSC approval in March and will break ground on Rook I in Saskatchewan this summer. At 30M lb U3O8/yr across an 11-year mine life, it would surpass Cameco's McArthur River and Cigar Lake as North America's largest uranium producer by output.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m at 0.83 g/t Au at Diablillos — Phase VI Underway, DFS Due Q2 2026 in Argentina
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX: ABRA) returned 171.0m grading 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag from 230m depth at Oculto East in Phase VI of drilling at Diablillos, Salta, Argentina. The broad intercept extends known mineralization; a Definitive Feasibility Study remains on schedule for Q2 2026.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss Open Pit in May — A$50M NRW Contract, First Concentrate Shipments Q4 2026
snippet: Core Lithium awarded a A$50M surface mining contract to NRW for the Grants pit at its Finniss project in Australia's NT; ore extraction begins May 2026 with processing starting July–September. The A$290M restart is anchored by a Glencore convertible note. First spodumene concentrate targeted Q4.

headline: Coeur Mining COMPLETES New Gold Acquisition — Gold Output Up 80%, $750M Buyback Launched
snippet: Coeur Mining (NYSE: CDE) closed its acquisition of New Gold Inc. on March 20, 2026, adding the Rainy River and New Afton mines in Ontario and B.C. Gold output guidance rises 80% to 680–815k oz for 2026, and Coeur launched a $750M share buyback alongside an inaugural semiannual dividend.

headline: Panama GREENLIGHTS Cobre Panama Ore Processing — First Quantum to Recover 70,000t Copper From Idle Stockpile
snippet: Panama's government on April 7 approved processing of the 38M-tonne ore stockpile at the shuttered Cobre Panamá mine — enough for ~70,000 tonnes of recoverable copper. First Quantum (TSX: FM) estimates $250M capex and 1,000+ jobs; this does not constitute a reopening of the mine.

headline: Copper's Next Supply Crisis Is STRUCTURAL, Not Cyclical — Analyst Makes the Case
snippet: A leading analyst argues the coming copper supply gap cannot be solved through a normal capex cycle: two decades of underinvestment in greenfield mines, worsening water constraints in Chile and DRC, and accelerating electrification demand point to a shortage that will outlast trade cycle volatility.

headline: USA RARE EARTH Invests in French Processor Carester — Transatlantic REE Supply Chain Takes Shape
snippet: USA Rare Earth has taken a strategic stake in Carester SAS, a French rare earth processing firm, as part of building a mine-to-magnet supply chain across the Atlantic. The move comes with nine months left before U.S. defense procurement rules require removal of Chinese REE content from supply chains.

headline: China Reports 'STUNNING' Critical Minerals Finds — REE Race With U.S. Intensifies
snippet: Chinese geological surveys have revealed major new rare earth and critical mineral discoveries, according to state media, as Beijing's hi-tech competition with Washington accelerates. China already controls roughly 70% of global REE production and 90% of refining capacity.

headline: GOLD-TO-SILVER Ratio Retreating From 105 Peak — Classic Bull Signal Flashing for Silver
snippet: After peaking above 105 in early April, the gold-to-silver ratio has begun contracting — a pattern that has historically preceded significant silver outperformance relative to gold. Analysts at GoldSeek call it a 'classic bull signal' for silver, given strong Chinese demand and shrinking exchange inventories.
